# 01 Healthy gross leasing activity in 9M 2024

4
contextual_situation: This chunk introduces the first major section of JLL's "India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024" report. It highlights the healthy gross leasing activity observed in the Indian retail market during the first nine months of 2024.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk from JLL's "India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024" report details the robust retail leasing activity across India during the first nine months of 2024. It highlights a healthy gross leasing of 5.3 million sq. ft. despite minimal new supply, with a focus on premium malls and Q3 2024 performance.

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# Robust leasing activity despite minimal supply

* Even with the addition of marginal supply of just over a million sq. ft., healthy gross leasing of 5.3 million sq. ft. was recorded during the first nine (9) months of 2024
* Retailers are expanding their footprint despite minimal new supply, signifying that retailers are not just relying solely on new supply for store openings. Premium malls have experienced significant tenant churning, with an influx of high-end and luxury brands. Mall developers have actively reconfigured existing spaces to accommodate these upscale retailers, demonstrating a shift towards more premium offerings in the retail landscape.
* Premium and larger-scale malls opened during the past 12 to 15 months have contributed significantly to increased retail space absorption. These establishments have attracted numerous new store openings, driving up overall space occupancy in the retail sector in the first nine months of 2024.
* Leasing activity for Q3 2024 remained strong, with 1.98 million sq. ft. of leasing recorded in the quarter.

### Retail leasing in 9M 2024 up by 8% on an annual basis

| Period | Area (mn sq. ft.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| 9M 2023 | 4.9 |
| 9M 2024 | 5.3 |

**Caption:** Retail leasing in 9M 2024 up by 8% on an annual basis. Axis: Area (mn sq. ft.).
Source: REIS, JLL Research and Retail

5

Context: This JLL report, published in November 2024, analyzes India's retail market trends and dynamics for the period of January to September 2024 across the top 7 cities. This specific chunk details the dominant segments in new retail space leasing activity and the share of domestic versus international retailers during 9M 2024.

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# Fashion & Apparel and F&B continue to dominate in new space take-up

* At 37%, the highest share in leasing activity was contributed by Fashion & Apparel
* F&B and Entertainment segments together represented ~30% of the leasing volume in the 9M 2024 period

### F&B and Entertainment segments together represented ~30% of the leasing volume in the 9M 2024 period

| Segment | Share (%) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Fashion & apparel | 37% |
| Food & beverage | 18% |
| Entertainment | 12% |
| Others | 7% |
| Home & furnishing | 6% |
| Footwear, bags & accessories | 5% |
| Daily needs & grocery | 5% |
| Jewellery | 4% |
| Beauty, cosmetics & wellness | 3% |
| CDIT | 3% |

Source: REIS, JLL Research and Retail

### Domestic retailers led the leasing activity in 2024

| Retailer Type | Share (%) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Domestic | 78% |
| International | 22% |

**Caption:** Domestic retailers led the leasing activity in 2024. Total leasing in 9M 2024: 5.3 mn sq ft.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This JLL report, titled "India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024", analyzes the retail market across India. This specific section details the strong leasing activity observed among D2C and luxury brands during the first nine months of 2024.

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# D2C and luxury brands witnessed strong leasing

* Super Premium, Bridge-to-Luxury and Luxury retail maintained its momentum, with high-end brands securing 0.15 million sq-ft of space in first 9 months of 2024. Majority of this leasing happened in Delhi NCR and Mumbai with a combined share of 76%.
* Continued interest from super premium and niche (luxury) retailers signals growing trend towards premiumization and confidence in the spending power of Indian consumers.
* Direct-to-Consumer (D2C) brands are also increasingly leasing spaces in physical retail destinations and had a share of about 7% in total gross leasing.
* Malls are increasingly becoming popular destinations for India’s D2C brands seeking to strengthen their connection with consumers through experiential retail.

### ~ 0.5 mn sq. ft. leased by D2C, Super Premium, Bridge-to-Luxury and Luxury brands

| Brand Category | Area (sq. ft.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Super premium, bridge-to-luxury and luxury brands | 150,000 |
| Direct to consumer (D2C) brands | 350,000 |

**Caption:** ~ 0.5 mn sq. ft. leased by D2C, Super Premium, Bridge-to-Luxury and Luxury brands. Axis: Area (sq. ft.).
Source: REIS, JLL Research and Retail

9

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk from JLL's 'India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024' report, published in November 2024, details the retail supply across India. It provides figures for operational supply in 9M 2024 and projections for the full year 2024, including specific data for Delhi NCR.

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# Annual supply for 2024 expected to surpass 1.9 mn sq. ft.

* Around **0.23 mn sq. ft.** of retail supply became operational in Q3 2024, taking the 9M 2024 supply addition to a total of 1.09 mn sq. ft.
* A new development with a leasable area of **225,000 sq. ft.** became operational in Delhi NCR during the quarter.
* Approximately **0.82 mn sq. ft.** of new retail supply is expected to come on stream by end of October-December quarter, taking up the 2024 full year supply to **1.9 million sq. ft.**

### Large mall completions in 9M 2023 led to a substantially higher supply base at 3.13 mn sq. ft.

| Period | Gross Leasable Area (mn sq. ft.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| 9M 2023 | 3.13 |
| 9M 2024 | 1.09 |

**Caption:** Gross Leasable Area (mn sq. ft.) by period.

### 1.1 mn sq. ft. of new supply became operational during 9M 2024

| Quarter | Gross Leasable Area (mn sq. ft.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Q1 2024 | 0.06 |
| Q2 2024 | 0.80 |
| Q3 2024 | 0.23 |
| Expected in Q4 2024 | 0.82 |

**Caption:** Gross Leasable Area (mn sq. ft.) by quarter for 2024.

Source: REIS, JLL Research and Retail

11

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# ~9.7 mn sq. ft. of new retail supply expected in 2025

* Majority of the supply in the next year will be in Delhi NCR, which is projected to account for 35%, followed by Hyderabad at 23% and Mumbai at 14%.

*During next year, Delhi NCR to witness the largest proportion (35%) of the supply*

### 9.7 million sq. ft. across 26 Grade A developments set to get operational in 2025

| City | Gross Leasable Area (mn sq. ft.) | Number of developments |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Delhi NCR | 3.36 | 7 |
| Hyderabad | 2.21 | 8 |
| Mumbai | 1.35 | 3 |
| Chennai | 1.25 | 3 |
| Bengaluru | 1.03 | 4 |
| Pune | 0.5 | 1 |

**Caption:** Gross Leasable Area (mn sq. ft.) and Number of developments by city for 2025.

Source: REIS, JLL Research and Retail

Context: This JLL report, titled "India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024" and published in November 2024, provides an overview of the retail real estate sector across India's top 7 cities. This specific chunk details the robust retail supply projected for India over the next five years, from Q4 2024 up to December 2028.

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# Robust supply of ~41 mn sq. ft. expected in next five years

* Approximately **40.8 mn sq. ft.** of new retail supply is expected to come on stream by 2028.
* Majority of this supply is expected in 2027 and 2028, consisting of some large malls spanning over 2.5 million sq. ft.

### Retail supply projection: Q4 2024 - Dec 2028

| Period | Area (mn sq. ft.) | Status |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 9M 2024 | 1.1 | Completions in 9M 2024 |
| 2024 F | 1.9 | Expected to get operational |
| 2025 | 9.7 | Expected to get operational |
| 2026 | 7.4 | Expected to get operational |
| 2027 | 9.9 | Expected to get operational |
| 2028 | 13.0 | Expected to get operational |

**Caption:** Retail supply projection (Area in mn sq. ft.) from 9M 2024 to 2028.

Source: REIS, JLL Research and Retail

final_embedding_text:
Context: This snippet, titled "Future outlook," is part of JLL's "India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024" report. It provides forward-looking statements and expectations for the Indian retail sector, including leasing activity, brand expansion, and investment trends.

Chunk Content: India Retail Market Trends and Dynamics: January to September 2024

# Future outlook

* Retail gross leasing across the top 7 cities is expected to surpass 7 mn sq. ft. by the end of the year, signaling continued growth and optimism in India’s retail sector.
* D2C brands will continue to expand strategically into physical retail locations to strengthen their connection with consumers. These brick-and-mortar stores act as experiential hubs, providing a seamless shopping experience to the consumers across multiple channels.
* India will retain its status as a prime destination for global brands aiming to broaden their store footprint. Strategic collaborations with global brands will remain a key growth strategy for leading Indian corporate retail chains.
* With the retail market’s growth, influx of institutional capital and domestic retailers expected to go for IPOs, retailers are set to expand their footprint.
* Robust supply coming in and Institutional Investors looking to acquire more retail assets, new retail led REIT listings are expected in the near future.

16
raw_text:

* Retail gross leasing across the top 7 cities is expected to surpass 7 mn sq. ft. by the end of the year, signaling continued growth and optimism in India’s retail sector.
* D2C brands will continue to expand strategically into physical retail locations to strengthen their connection with consumers. These brick-and-mortar stores act as experiential hubs, providing a seamless shopping experience to the consumers across multiple channels.
* India will retain its status as a prime destination for global brands aiming to broaden their store footprint. Strategic collaborations with global brands will remain a key growth strategy for leading Indian corporate retail chains.
* With the retail market’s growth, influx of institutional capital and domestic retailers expected to go for IPOs, retailers are set to expand their footprint.
* Robust supply coming in and Institutional Investors looking to acquire more retail assets, new retail led REIT listings are expected in the near future.

### About JLL
For over 200 years, JLL (NYSE: JLL), a leading global commercial real estate and investment management company, has helped clients buy, build, occupy, manage and invest in a variety of commercial, industrial, hotel, residential and retail properties. A Fortune 500® company with annual revenue of $20.8 billion and operations in over 80 countries around the world, our more than 111,000 employees bring the power of a global platform combined with local expertise. Driven by our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world, we help our clients, people and communities SEE A BRIGHTER WAY^SM. JLL is the brand name, and a registered trademark, of Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated. For further information, visit jll.com.

### Research enquiries
**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head
Research and REIS
samantak.das@jll.com

### Research authors
**Abhishek Vohra**
Assistant Manager
Research and REIS

### Business enquiries

**Sharad Nagpal**
Senior Director and Head - Retail Consulting and International Business Development, India

**Shweta Kakkar**
Director
Retail Data Specialist and Consulting

For other business enquiries, please write to **marketing.india@jll.com**
